title: Tri-modal nucleic acid delivery systems
abstract: Provided herein are nucleic acid delivery compositions, surfactants, kits comprising said materials, methods and uses thereof, and methods for the preparation thereof. In particular, nucleic acid delivery compositions described herein may include tri-modal nucleic acid delivery compositions which may comprise at least one peptide enhancer, at least one surfactant, and at least one helper lipid. By way of example, certain of the tri-modal nucleic acid delivery compositions described herein include a peptide enhancer; a surfactant which is a functionalized cationic gemini surfactant; and a helper lipid which is a neutral lipid such as DOPE.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A tri -modal nucleic acid delivery composition compri at least one peptide enhancer; at least one surfactant; and at least one helper lipid. 2. The tri-modal nucleic acid delivery composition according to claim 1, wherein the peptide enhancer is zwitterionic, cationic, and/or comprises at least one histidine, lysine, or arginine residue. 3. The tri-modal nucleic acid delivery composition according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the peptide enhancer comprises an RGD sequence motif. 4. The tri-modal nucleic acid delivery composition according to any one of claims 1-3, wherein the peptide enhancer comprises an amino acid sequence of PA (GRGDSPG; SEQ ID NO: 1), P B (H(R) 3 H(R) 3 HG; SEQ ID NO: 2), P C (GRGDSPGH(R) 3 H(R) 3 HG; SEQ ID NO: 3), P D ((H) 5 ; SEQ ID NO: 4), P E (GRGDSPG(H)s; SEQ ID NO: 5), P F ((H) 2 R(H) 7 R(H) 3 G; SEQ ID NO: 6), P G (GRGDSPG(H) 2 R(H) 7 R(H) 3 G; SEQ ID NO: 7), or GRGDSP (SEQ ID NO: 16). 5. The tri-modal nucleic acid delivery composition according to any one of claims 1-4, wherein the surfactant comprises a fusogenic surfactant. 6. The tri -modal nucleic acid delivery composition according to any one of claims 1-5, wherein the surfactant comprises a cationic gemini surfactant 7. The tri-modal nucleic acid delivery composition according to claim 6, wherein the cationic gemini surfactant comprises two monomelic surfactants linked by a spacer group. 8. The tri-modal nucleic acid delivery composition according to claim 7, wherein the gemini surfactant has the formula m-s-m, where m represents the number of alkyl tail carbon atoms of each monomelic surfactant, and s represents the number of atoms in the spacer group. 9. The tri-modal nucleic acid delivery composition according to any one of claims 1-8, wherein the surfactant is functionalized with a functional moiety. 10. The tri-modal nucleic acid delivery composition according to claim 9, wherein the surfactant is a functionalized with the functional moiety by covalent attachment, optionally though a linker. 11. The tri-modal nucleic acid delivery composition according to claim 10, wherein the surfactant is a cationic gemini surfactant, the surfactant is functionalized with the functional moiety by covalent attachment to a nitrogen atom in the surfactant optionally through a linker, and the nitrogen atom in the surfactant is a nitrogen atom of a spacer group of the cationic gemini surfactant. 12. The tri-modal nucleic acid delivery composition according to any one of claims 1-11, wherein the surfactant is an m-7 H-m cationic gemini surfactant or a derivative thereof. 13. The tri-modal nucleic acid delivery composition according to claim 12, wherein each m is independently an integerâ¥12 andâ¤18, s isâ¥3 andâ¤7, or both. 14. The tri-modal nucleic acid delivery composition of any one of claims 1-13, wherein the surfactant is functionalized with a functional moiety which comprises an imidazole- containing functional group, a thiol-containing functional group, a linear RGD-containing peptide functional group, a polyhistidine-containing peptide functional group, a bifunctional RGD-polyhistidine-containing peptide functional group, a zwitterionic and/or cationic arginine-rich peptide functional group, or any combination thereof. 15. The tri-modal nucleic acid delivery composition of claim 14, wherein the functional moiety comprises: 16. The tri-modal nucleic acid delivery composition of any one of claims 1-15, wherein the helper lipid comprises a neutral helper lipid. 17. The tri-modal nucleic acid delivery composition of any one of claims 1-16, wherein the helper lipid comprises DOPE (l,2-dioleyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphoethanolamine), DPPC (1,2- dipalmitoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine), a derivative thereof, or any combination thereof. 18. The tri-modal nucleic acid delivery composition of any one of claims 1-17, further comprising a nucleic acid for delivery to a cell. 19. The tri-modal nucleic acid delivery composition of claim 18, wherein the nucleic acid is a plasmid, expression vector, therapeutic nucleic acid, or another nucleic acid molecule. 20. The tri-modal nucleic acid delivery composition of any one of claims 1-19, having a cationic surfactant/nucleic acid charge ratio (p) of p < 3. 21. The tri-modal nucleic acid delivery composition of any one of claims 1-20, having a helper lipid/surfactant molar ratio (r) of r < 10. 22. The tri-modal nucleic acid delivery composition of any one of claims 1-21, having a molar concentration of peptide enhancer (Mp) of Mp < 1000 Î¼Î, a molar concentration of surfactant (MG) of MCâ¤ 46 Î¼Î, and a molar concentration of helper lipid (ML) of MLâ¤300 Î¼Î. 23. The tri-modal nucleic acid delivery composition of any one of claims 1-22, having a surface charge (Î¶ potential) of -60 mVâ¤ Î¶â¤ 60 mV. 24. The tri-modal nucleic acid delivery composition of any one of claims 1-23, having a particle size of > 80 nm and < 350 nm. 25. A kit for delivering a nucleic acid to a cell, the kit comprising a tri-modal nucleic acid delivery composition according to any one of claims 1-24 and, optionally, instructions for formulating the nucleic acid with the tri-modal nucleic acid delivery composition. 26. A method of delivering a nucleic acid to a cell, said method comprising: generating a delivery vehicle comprising the nucleic acid by formulating the nucleic acid with the tri-modal nucleic acid delivery composition according to any one of claims 1-24; and administering the delivery vehicle to the cell. 27. Use of the tri-modal nucleic acid delivery composition according to any one of claims 1- 24 for delivering a nucleic acid to a cell. 28. A method of preparing a nucleic acid for delivery to a cell, said method comprising: formulating the nucleic acid with the tri-modal nucleic acid delivery composition according to any one of claims 1-24. 29. A gemini surfactant comprising two monomeric surfactants linked by a spacer group, the 

 gemini surfactant being covalently functionalized with a functional moiety. 30. The gemini surfactant of claim 29, wherein the functional moiety comprises an imidazole-containing functional group, a thiol-containing functional group, a linear RGD- containing peptide functional group, a polyhistidine-containing peptide functional group, a bifunctional RGD-polyhistidine-containing peptide functional group, a zwitterionic and/or cationic arginine-rich peptide functional group, or any combination thereof. 31. The gemini surfactant according to claim 29 or 30, wherein the gemini surfactant comprises the structure of formula II: wherein at least one of RA, RB, and Rc of a first monomeric surfactant portion comprises an alkyl-based tail having mi carbon atoms, and the remaining of RA, RB, and Rc are substituents, such as alkyl substituents, which cause the nitrogen to which they are attached to be quaternary; wherein at least one of RF, RG, and RH of a second monomeric surfactant portion comprises an alkyl-based tail having m 2 carbon atoms, and the remaining of RF, RG, and RH are substituents, such as alkyl substituents, which cause the nitrogen to which they are attached to be quaternary; and wherein spacer -RD-N(R)-RE- links the first and second monomeric surfactant portions through their respective quaternary nitrogens, RD and RE each represent an alkyl-based group or derivative thereof, R represents the functional moiety and is covalently joined to the nitrogen of the spacer, and s represents the total number of spacer atoms along the 

 shortest linear path running between the quaternary nitrogens of the first and second monomeric surfactant portions. 32. The gemini surfactant according to any one of claims 29-31, wherein the gemini surfactant comprises the structure of formula III: wherein 12 < m < 18, and m may be the same, or different, between the two monomeric surfactant portions; wherein s is 7; and wherein R is the functional moiety. 33. The gemini surfactant according to claim 32, wherein m is 12 or 18 and is the same for both monomeric surfactant portions. 34. The gemini surfactant according to any one of claims 29-33, wherein the functional moiety comprises any one of R 1 -R 10 as defined in claim 15. 35. A composition comprising the gemini surfactant according to any one of claims 29-34, and at least one of a peptide enhancer, a helper lipid, a nucleic acid, a pharmaceutically acceptable excipient, diluent, or buffer. 36. A kit for delivering a nucleic acid to a cell, the kit comprising the gemini surfactant according to any one of claims 29-34, and, optionally, one or more of a peptide enhancer, a helper lipid, a nucleic acid, or instructions for formulating the nucleic acid with the gemini surfactant. 37. Use of the gemini surfactant according to any one of claims 29-34 for delivering a nucleic acid to a cell. 38. The use according to claim 37, wherein the gemini surfactant is for use in combination with at least one peptide enhancer and/or at least one helper lipid. 39. A method of delivering a nucleic acid to a cell, said method comprising: formulating the nucleic acid with the gemini surfactant according to any one of claims 29-34; and administering the formulated nucleic acid to the cell. 40. The method of claim 39, wherein the formulating step additionally comprises formulating the nucleic acid with a peptide enhancer and/or a helper lipid. 41. A method of preparing a nucleic acid for delivery to a cell, said method comprising: formulating the nucleic acid with the gemini surfactant according to any one of claims 29-34. 42. The method according to claim 41, wherein the formulating step additionally comprises formulating the nucleic acid with a peptide enhancer and/or a helper lipid.
